subject: English
title: For Anne Gregory – Easy Short Notes

key_points: The poem 'For Anne Gregory' is written by W. B. Yeats
The poem is written in the form of a dialogue
A young man says that people admire Anne Gregory because of her beautiful yellow hair
Anne Gregory says she can dye her hair different colours
She wants someone to love her for her true personality
The poem discusses the difference between outer beauty and inner qualities
The young man represents common social beliefs about beauty
Anne Gregory represents the desire to be valued for inner identity
An old religious man says only God can love someone for themselves alone
The poem encourages readers to value inner character rather than appearance
characters: Anne Gregory – A young woman who wishes to be loved for her true self rather than her beauty
The Young Man – Represents the common belief that people are attracted by physical beauty
The Religious Man – Introduces the idea that divine love is pure and unconditional
themes: True love should value inner character
Society often judges people based on appearance
Divine love is pure and unconditional
literary_devices: Metaphor – 'Honey-coloured ramparts' refers to Anne Gregory’s yellow hair
Symbolism – Anne Gregory’s hair symbolises physical beauty
Dialogue – The poem is presented as a conversation between two speakers
Contrast – The poem contrasts outer beauty with inner personality
exam_tips: Remember that the poem is written by W. B. Yeats
Mention the phrase 'honey-coloured ramparts' when discussing imagery
Explain the contrast between physical beauty and inner qualities
Include the religious man's statement about divine love
Focus on the central message that true love values inner personality
